note: Frozen PROFESSOR-APPS posting corpus v3 (real faculty hiring units; full-length ads). The agent fetches this file and classifies every posting by id against the candidate CV. No fit verdicts here. Department title is not a shortcut, and each ad's decisive detail (position type, credential, or the nature of the research program) sits in the Responsibilities/Qualifications sections, not the summary - read each ad in full.

postings.description:
University of Connecticut
Department of Statistics - Assistant Professor in Residence

University of Connecticut invites applications for a faculty position in the Department of Statistics, with an anticipated start in the next academic year. We seek an outstanding colleague who will contribute to the unit's mission and engage with our broad activity touching statistics teaching and related quantitative areas. The successful candidate will join a collegial, intellectually ambitious community and help shape the unit's next decade of growth, and will be supported by mentoring, seminar series, and cross-campus collaboration from the first day.

The Department of Statistics is a growing unit at University of Connecticut with strong ties to neighboring programs, ample computing and data resources, dedicated staff support, and a record of student placement into academia, government, and industry. Colleagues collaborate across departments and centers, and the unit prioritizes a welcoming environment in which a new faculty member can thrive.

The surrounding region offers a high quality of life, a reasonable cost of living, and easy access to cultural and outdoor amenities; University of Connecticut provides a comprehensive benefits package, dual-career support, and relocation assistance. The institution's strategic plan emphasizes data-rich, interdisciplinary scholarship, and new colleagues are encouraged to build collaborations that cross traditional boundaries.

Responsibilities: the position joins an active unit and contributes to its shared mission and seminar life. Day to day, the colleague will teach four course sections each semester across the statistics teaching sequence, coordinate multi-section service courses, hold extensive office hours, advise undergraduate majors, supervise teaching assistants, and contribute to assessment and curriculum committees. The central, defining duty of this position is excellent, high-enrollment classroom instruction and student support.

Preferred qualifications include a demonstrated ability to attract external support, a commitment to inclusive and effective mentoring, and the capacity to collaborate across disciplinary boundaries. We particularly welcome candidates who will engage undergraduate and graduate students from a range of backgrounds and who can contribute to the unit's service and outreach.

Qualifications: a PhD (or a master's with extensive teaching experience) in a relevant field and demonstrated teaching excellence. This is a full-time, NON-TENURE-TRACK teaching appointment (Teaching Professor / Lecturer; renewable); it carries no start-up package and no independent-research expectation, and is evaluated on instruction and student support.

To apply, upload a curriculum vitae, a cover letter, separate statements of research and teaching, and contact information for three referees. Applications received by the priority deadline will receive full consideration, and the search remains open until filled.

University of Connecticut is committed to building a diverse and inclusive faculty and strongly encourages applications from candidates of all backgrounds and lived experiences.
